questions.question: What is the fundamental function of a logic inverter (NOT gate) in digital circuits?
options: Adds two input bits together.
Stores a single bit of data for a clock cycle.
Reverses the logic state (0 to 1, or 1 to 0) of its input.
Selects one of multiple input signals based on a control input.
Generates a periodic clock signal for synchronous operations.

questions.question: In a CPU, what is the primary function of the Program Counter (PC)?
options: Holds the result of the last arithmetic or logical operation.
Stores the memory address of the next instruction to be fetched and executed.
Manages the data flow between the processor's registers and the Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU).
Keeps track of the memory address of the current operand being processed.
Controls the timing and sequencing of all instruction execution phases.

questions.question: In the digital IC design flow, what is the primary objective of Static Timing Analysis (STA)?
options: To verify the functional correctness of the design by simulating its behavior with extensive test vectors.
To determine if the circuit will meet all its timing requirements (e.g., clock frequency, setup/hold times) across various operating conditions without needing to run simulations.
To identify and fix logical errors in the Register Transfer Level (RTL) code before it is synthesized.
To extract parasitic resistances and capacitances from the physical layout for highly accurate post-layout simulations.
To automatically generate the physical layout (place and route) of the design based on the gate-level netlist.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB) in a computer system?
options: To store frequently accessed data blocks for the CPU, reducing access time to main memory.
To accelerate the translation of virtual memory addresses to physical memory addresses.
To maintain data consistency across multiple CPU caches in a multi-processor system.
To provide fast access to the most recently executed instructions.
To temporarily store data during direct memory access (DMA) operations between peripherals and main memory.

questions.question: In the digital IC design flow, what is the process called that converts a high-level Hardware Description Language (HDL) description (e.g., Verilog RTL) into an optimized gate-level netlist?
options: Verification
Fabrication
Synthesis
Physical Layout
Post-Layout Simulation
